name: CLI token baseline
summary: CLI agents pay primarily for command text, stdout/stderr, and short system guidance—not for dozens of tool schemas. Scalekit reports hundreds to low thousands of tokens per GitHub task via gh. Discovery via --help or compact skill files (~800 tokens) can reduce tool-call count versus naive CLI. Trade-off: ambient credentials and weak audit versus MCP OAuth.

key_metrics.composability: pipes and shell scripts in one turn

methodology_notes: Baseline from Scalekit harness (gh, bash). Practitioner posts (Afroze Amjad, Joel Claw CLI-for-agents) align on JSON --output and schema commands for agent-native CLIs. GAX eval uses chars/4 token heuristic on gh stdout.

primary_sources.title: Joel Claw CLI design for AI agents

implications_for_gax: GAX matches CLI invocation size (short commands) while adding envelope v1 and audit_id. surface=model projection keeps response tokens comparable to filtered CLI JSON. Inner-loop dev should default to GAX or gh-with-cap, not naive MCP.
corrections_to_repo_docs: None required if 4x-32x and CLI token ranges already cite Scalekit.
